summary: Draft candidate package for PerfCalculateActualUtilization under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power. The clean Win25H2Clean baseline confirmed the current default, the repo power notes carry an exact docs hit, the shared string batch found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe hit, the shared Ghidra batch produced reviewable xref artifacts, and the tools-hardened lightweight ETW follow-up on RegProbe-Baseline-ToolsHardened-20260330 captured an exact runtime read for PerfCalculateActualUtilization. App surfacing remains a separate product decision from evidence classification.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Docs/power/power.md:181 shows `PerfCalculateActualUtilization` with observed literal `1` in the repo power notes.
validation_proof.notes: The docs-first triage and phase-0 baseline agree on the current Win25H2Clean baseline value for PerfCalculateActualUtilization. App surfacing is tracked separately from evidence classification.

setting.name: Perf Calculate Actual Utilization
setting.casual_explanation: Perf Calculate Actual Utilization is a low-level power-manager registry value under the main Power key.
setting.why_people_change_it: Researchers track this value when reviewing processor performance accounting and utilization heuristics.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Cross-layer evidence now converges for this raw power-manager value: docs, current-build static hits, Ghidra review, and an exact runtime read are all attached.
setting.professional_notes: Win25H2Clean phase-0 existence confirmed PerfCalculateActualUtilization on the live baseline with preview value 1. The docs-first triage then tied the value to exact repo documentation hits, led by Docs/power/power.md:181. A shared current-build string batch found an exact hit in C:\Windows\System32\ntoskrnl.exe, and the shared Ghidra batch on the same binary preserved a reviewable xref/decompile artifact set for the docs-first power-control family. Earlier shell-safe Procmon lanes on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 tightened the runtime story but did not capture an exact read. The tools-hardened lightweight ETW follow-up then reran the candidate on RegProbe-Baseline-ToolsHardened-20260330 and captured an exact runtime read on the short trigger ETW lane while the guest stayed shell-healthy.

allowed_values.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The current clean VM baseline exposes PerfCalculateActualUtilization=1.

targets.notes: This candidate is tracked as a raw kernel-facing value, not as a published end-user tweak profile.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app uses the documented PERFBOOSTMODE command surface and does not ship PerfCalculateActualUtilization as a standalone raw registry value.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline

states.rationale: Keep the currently observed baseline value (1) while this raw control stays in research packaging only.

windows_defaults.notes: Observed directly from the 96-key phase-0 existence batch on Win25H2Clean.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed baseline

states.rationale: Track the observed baseline value without turning it into a shipped preset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the repo to track the live baseline and current evidence for this raw power-manager value.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a supported end-user tweak or a documented Microsoft control surface.
tradeoffs: Keeps the candidate research-only and non-actionable.

evidence.title: Repo power notes for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: Repo power notes contain an exact entry for PerfCalculateActualUtilization, led by Docs/power/power.md:181.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
evidence.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed PerfCalculateActualUtilization exists on the clean baseline with observed value 1.

evidence.title: Shared docs-first string triage for current-build ntoskrnl
evidence.summary: Shared string triage found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe string hit for PerfCalculateActualUtilization.

evidence.title: Shared Ghidra xref batch for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The shared ntoskrnl Ghidra batch produced a reviewable string/xref artifact set for PerfCalculateActualUtilization; some values resolved naturally while several remained forced-boundary fallback blocks.

evidence.title: Shared clean-baseline guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log completed shell-safe on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 but did not capture an exact runtime read for PerfCalculateActualUtilization.

evidence.title: Guest-processed post-boot Procmon trigger batch for remaining docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: A guest-processed post-boot Procmon lane ran powercfg queries, powercfg /energy, CPU stress, and profile-specific follow-up commands on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329. It captured path-level Control\\Power activity but still did not capture an exact runtime read for PerfCalculateActualUtilization.

evidence.title: Tools-hardened lightweight ETW follow-up for remaining docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The tools-hardened lightweight runtime lane on RegProbe-Baseline-ToolsHardened-20260330 captured an exact runtime read for PerfCalculateActualUtilization. The guest stayed shell-healthy and the host recovered the compact phase summary even though guestVar publication stayed unreliable.

decision.why: PerfCalculateActualUtilization now has converged cross-layer evidence on RegProbe-Baseline-ToolsHardened-20260330: phase-0 baseline existence, exact repo-doc hits, current-build ntoskrnl string corroboration, reviewable Ghidra artifacts, prior shell-safe Procmon lanes, and an exact runtime read captured by the tools-hardened lightweight ETW follow-up. App mapping is tracked separately and does not block evidence classification.
